Build workflows that think.

NEXUS connects your stack with AI that understands your business logic.

START BUILDING → WATCH DEMO — 3 MIN

issues: Classified as generic FLEX_COLUMN — missed HERO_PATTERN entirely
hero-eyebrow → TEXT_WIDGET (correct but no class applied)
h1 with em/span → HEADING_WIDGET but lost inline styles
hero-bottom → FLEX_ROW (correct)
btn-primary and btn-ghost → both classified as TEXT_WIDGET not BUTTON_WIDGET
_css_classes missing on all widgets
No min_height set on hero container
No companion CSS for -webkit-text-stroke or italic outline

children.reason: eyebrow has ::before line decoration needed in CSS
ghost link needs ::before border-bottom styling — Text Editor gives class access

confidence_calibration.button_detection_rule: a[href] with padding + background-color → BUTTON_WIDGET confidence +0.3
confidence_calibration.min_height_rule: section with min-height ≥ 80vh or height:100vh → set min_height in settings
confidence_calibration.eyebrow_rule: div/span with font-size ≤ 12px + letter-spacing ≥ .1em + before heading → HTML_WIDGET (needs ::before line)

START BUILDING → WATCH DEMO

issues: BEM block 'hero-section' not matched to HERO_PATTERN — vocabulary lookup missed 'hero-section'
BEM modifiers (--dark, --xl) not parsed — modifier styles lost
btn--primary modifier not matched to BUTTON_WIDGET — only 'btn' class seen
hero-section__headline-italic (BEM element) not identified as em-equivalent
Confidence low because block name 'hero-section' not in direct vocabulary (vs just 'hero')

corrected_output.note: Same as SIM-001 corrected output structurally
bem_parsing_rules_applied: Modifier: --dark → background colour from CSS resolved, applied to container
Modifier: --xl → large font size resolved from CSS
Block: btn, Modifier: --primary → BUTTON_WIDGET with primary styling
Block: btn, Modifier: --ghost → TEXT_EDITOR (ghost link pattern)

delta.root_cause: BEM block name used pattern variation 'hero-section' not 'hero'
delta.fix: Expand vocabulary matching to include BEM block names with suffix variations: hero*, *-hero, hero-*, *-header, *-banner, *-above-fold

bem_parsing_additions.modifier_resolution: Always resolve --modifier CSS from stylesheet and apply to Elementor settings

confidence_calibration.bem_block_hero_match: BEM block name in hero vocabulary → HERO_PATTERN confidence +0.8
confidence_calibration.bem_modifier_resolution: Always resolve modifier CSS before settings extraction

AI PLATFORM · V4.0

Build workflows that think.

Connect your stack...

START BUILDING → WATCH DEMO — 3 MIN

issues: No semantic class names — all utility classes
min-h-screen not parsed as min-height:100vh → HERO signal missed
font-black not mapped to font-weight:900
text-[120px] arbitrary value not parsed
bg-[#0a0a12] arbitrary colour not extracted
before:content-[''] before:w-10 — Tailwind JIT pseudo-element classes not understood
[-webkit-text-stroke:1px_rgba(245,243,238,.4)] arbitrary property not parsed
CTA link classified as TEXT_WIDGET (not BUTTON_WIDGET) — no CSS resolves to button styles without framework
Overall: 31% confidence, everything falls to HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX

tailwind_resolver_actions: Detect Tailwind: class count per element > 5 with known utility prefixes
Load Tailwind CSS file if present OR use JIT simulation from class names
Resolve each utility class to its CSS property:value
before:content-[''] before:w-10 before:h-px before:bg-[#c8ff00] → ::before { content:''; width:40px; height:1px; background:#c8ff00 }
Output: computed inline style string per element, then run standard pipeline
corrected_output.result: Same as SIM-001 after Tailwind resolution

delta.root_cause: Tailwind utility classes carry no semantic information — need resolved CSS first
delta.fix: Pre-processing pass: Tailwind Resolver converts utilities to computed styles before main pipeline
tailwind_detection_signals: Classes match known Tailwind prefixes: flex, grid, min-h-, text-, font-, bg-, px-, py-, gap-, items-, justify-, tracking-, leading-, max-w-, border-
Presence of arbitrary value syntax: text-[...], bg-[...], [property:value]
Presence of modifier prefix syntax: before:, after:, hover:, focus:, sm:, md:, lg:

simulations.input_html:
AI PLATFORM · V4.0

Build workflows that think.

Connect your stack...

START BUILDING → WATCH DEMO

issues: ALL classes obfuscated — zero semantic signal from class names
sc-* classes are Styled Components hashes — meaningless
_2kX9p, _mNqR — CSS Modules hashes — meaningless
data-section='1' is the only structural hint
Pipeline falls through all vocabulary checks, all BEM checks
Confidence 0.22 — below all thresholds → HTML_WIDGET_COMPLEX

steps: Step 1: data-section='1' → FIRST_SECTION signal (+0.4 to hero confidence)
Step 2: CSS resolution — _2kX9p has min-height:100vh → HERO signal (+0.85)
Step 3: _7yPl8 has font-size:120px, font-weight:800 → DISPLAY_HEADING signal
Step 4: h1 tag type → has_h1 signal (+0.9)
Step 5: DOM position — first child of h1 class element with em → heading with styled inline
Step 6: sc-bdVTJa has background-color:#c8ff00 + padding:18px 36px → BUTTON_WIDGET signal (+0.85)
Step 7: sc-fnykZn has border-bottom + no background → GHOST_LINK signal
Assign nx- prefix classes based on structural role, NOT original class names
corrected_output.key_insight: When all class names are obfuscated, CSS property values ARE the signal. Background-color + padding + cursor:pointer = button regardless of class name.

delta.root_cause: Zero semantic signal from class names — must rely entirely on CSS property fingerprints
delta.fix: CSS Property Fingerprinting module: classify elements by their resolved style profiles

required: is h1-h3 tag OR has largest font on page

data_attribute_hints.data-section: section index — data-section='1' → likely hero/first section
data_attribute_hints.aria-label: accessibility label often reveals semantic role
data_attribute_hints.role: landmark roles: banner → header/hero, contentinfo → footer, main → main content

AI PLATFORM · V4.0

Build workflows that think.

Connect your stack...

START → WATCH DEMO

issues: Partial recognition: 'hero' class found → hero confidence boosted but not certain
'eyebrow' class found → EYEBROW_PATTERN recognised
Tailwind classes on same elements causing conflicting signals
sc-* hash classes adding noise
inline style clamp() — parsed partially
btn-primary recognised but sc-cZFQDh hash class also present
Overall: partial match, mixed signals, medium confidence

corrected_output.strategy: PRIORITY_STACK: semantic > BEM > pattern fingerprint > Tailwind > obfuscated
resolution: data-section='hero' → HERO_PATTERN confidence 0.95 immediately (data attribute override)
Tailwind classes resolved in parallel: flex-col, justify-end, etc → confirm layout
sc-* hashes ignored (hash classes always lowest priority)
class 'ghost-link' → TEXT_EDITOR with ghost link CSS
inline style clamp() → extract to companion CSS, use 120px as Elementor fallback

delta.root_cause: Mixed signals from multiple naming systems on same elements
delta.fix: Priority stack: when multiple naming systems present on same element, apply them in order and merge signals

priority_resolution_rules.1_data_attributes: Highest priority — developer explicitly stated role
priority_resolution_rules.3_bem_blocks: High priority — BEM block name vocabulary match
priority_resolution_rules.4_pattern_fingerprints: Medium-high — CSS property profile matches known component
priority_resolution_rules.5_tailwind_resolved: Medium — resolved utility values add to confidence
priority_resolution_rules.6_obfuscated_hashes: Lowest — ignore for classification, may contain style info

clamp_handling.rule: Extract min value as Elementor font_size setting, put full clamp() in companion CSS

issues: Grid detected — correct
grid-template-columns: repeat(12,1fr) → confused: 12 columns, not 3
Naive engine creates 12-column Elementor grid instead of recognising the 5/4/3 fr pattern
grid-row:span 5 and span 3 → ignored (naive engine doesn't compute implied fr)
card-tag (div with small caps) → TEXT_WIDGET not HTML_WIDGET
.terminal div → classified as FLEX_COLUMN not HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED
.pipeline div → classified as FLEX_COLUMN not HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED
All cards get equal height (Elementor default) → bento asymmetry lost

grid_resolution.analysis: 12-column grid but cards use spans of 5, 4, 3 — the actual visual columns are 3 groups
grid_resolution.simplification: Simplify to 3-column grid with fr proportions: 5fr 4fr 3fr
grid_resolution.reasoning: Elementor grid_columns_fr matches the visual proportions, not the underlying unit count

delta.root_cause_1: 12-column repeat() grid not simplified to visual column proportions
delta.root_cause_2: grid-row spans not translated to grid_row_start/end
delta.root_cause_3: Animated sub-components (terminal, pipeline) not detected as HTML_WIDGET
delta.fix_1: Grid simplification algorithm: find GCD of all span values, reduce to fr proportions
delta.fix_2: Span-to-explicit converter: grid-column:span N → compute start/end from DOM position
delta.fix_3: Animation sub-detector: check children of grid cards for animation signals

grid_simplification_algorithm.step3: Check if they sum to the total columns: 5+4+3 = 12 ✓
grid_simplification_algorithm.step4: If yes: use spans as fr values directly: '5fr 4fr 3fr'
grid_simplification_algorithm.step5: If partial: find the most common grouping pattern

span_to_placement_algorithm.description: Given DOM order and span values, compute grid_column/row_start/end
rules: Track current column and row position as we iterate children
grid-column: span N means 'place at current position, span N columns'
grid-row: span N means 'span N rows from current row'
After placing, advance current column by span value
If current column + next span > total columns: wrap to next row

issues: Grid correctly detected — 3 columns
price-card → FLEX_COLUMN container (correct structure)
price-plan → TEXT_WIDGET (should be with class nx-price-plan)
price-amount with <sup> → TEXT_WIDGET (correct but loses sup styling)
price-period → TEXT_WIDGET (correct)
price-features <ul> → LIST_WIDGET (actually wrong — should be HTML_WIDGET for arrow styling)
price-badge with position:absolute → missed — not classified at all (absolute children often skipped)
featured card background:#c8ff00 → correct colour extracted

corrected_output.each_card: FLEX_COLUMN inner container with specific classes
corrected_output.price-badge: HTML_WIDGET inside card — position:absolute in companion CSS, requires container position:relative
corrected_output.price-plan: TEXT_EDITOR (not heading — it's a label, not a title)
corrected_output.price-amount: HTML_WIDGET — contains <sup> formatting that TEXT_EDITOR can't style
corrected_output.price-features: HTML_WIDGET — requires custom arrow styling and spacing
corrected_output.button: BUTTON_WIDGET — <button> elements are buttons regardless of tag

delta.root_cause_1: <button> tag not recognised as BUTTON_WIDGET (only <a> was in button detection)
delta.root_cause_2: Absolutely positioned children not classified (skipped by traversal)
delta.root_cause_3: <ul> with <li> text items classified as LIST_WIDGET but needs HTML_WIDGET for custom arrow styling
delta.fix_1: Button detection: both <a> AND <button> elements → BUTTON_WIDGET if they have button-like styling
delta.fix_2: Absolute position children: classify them as HTML_WIDGET if their parent is a positioned container
delta.fix_3: Feature list detection: ul.price-features → HTML_WIDGET if companion CSS needed for custom bullets

extracted_signals.button_tag_rule: Both <a> and <button> elements qualify for BUTTON_WIDGET classification
extracted_signals.absolute_child_rule: Children with position:absolute inside a positioned card → HTML_WIDGET (badge, label)
extracted_signals.feature_list_rule: ul inside pricing card → HTML_WIDGET if list items need custom icon styling (not standard bullets)

issues: Grid detected correctly
stat-cell → FLEX_COLUMN container (wrong — should be HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED)
JS IntersectionObserver detected but not associated with specific cells
stat-num with data-target attribute → TEXT_WIDGET (misses animation dependency)
::before progress line on .stat-cell → missed entirely
count span with data-target → not identified as animated counter

corrected_output.reason: Every stat-cell has JS animation dependency. The script modifies DOM text content dynamically. The ::before progress line is part of the visual. Making each cell native would require the count-up JS to still run — creating a dependency on an external script. Cleanest solution: entire stats section as one HTML widget containing all cells, CSS, and JS.

html_widget_structure.editability: 4/10 — stat numbers and labels can be edited in the HTML widget source

delta.root_cause: JS animation on DOM text content → entire component must be HTML_WIDGET
delta.rule: If a script modifies the text content of an element via querySelector + textContent, that element CANNOT be a native TEXT_EDITOR widget — it must be HTML_WIDGET
delta.detection: Scan scripts for: textContent, innerHTML, innerText, .value modification on elements that would otherwise be native widgets

js_dom_mutation_rule.trigger: Script contains querySelector + textContent/innerHTML on element that would be native widget
js_dom_mutation_rule.action: Classify that element (and its closest container) as HTML_WIDGET
js_dom_mutation_rule.exception: If the mutation is only for class toggling (adding CSS classes) — keep native widget, put JS in Global Setup

testi-name + testi-role → separate TEXT_WIDGETs in separate containers (over-fragmented)

corrected_output.testi-author: HTML_WIDGET — avatar circle + name + role as a unit
reason_for_author_html: Avatar circle needs border-radius:50%, specific background-color, flex centering
These are not settable on a native Text Editor or Heading widget

Editability note in report: 'Author name/role can be edited in HTML widget source'

delta.root_cause: Avatar circle component not recognised → author block over-fragmented into native widgets that cannot reproduce the circular avatar
delta.fix: Avatar Circle Fingerprint: round element with initials text, specific bg-color → HTML_WIDGET parent

avatar_circle_fingerprint.action: If avatar circle detected in author block → entire author block → HTML_WIDGET

extracted_signals.testimonial_author_block_rule: Author block = avatar circle + name + role → always HTML_WIDGET regardless of naming convention

issues: position:fixed → should immediately trigger HTML_WIDGET_FIXED
Scroll event listener → JS-dependent class toggle
nav-links <ul> → LIST_WIDGET (correct structure but loses nav styling)
nav-logo with <span> for accent colour → TEXT_EDITOR (loses span colour)
Classified as native FLEX_ROW container → will not be fixed positioned in Elementor

reason: position:fixed on nav → ALWAYS HTML_WIDGET (body-level injection needed)
Scroll class toggle JS → belongs in Global Setup script, not nav widget
Nav must be the SECOND element in the template (after Global Setup)
All nav styles in widget-internal <style> block
Scroll listener in Global Setup IIFE targets the nav widget by ID

delta.fix: HARD RULE: position:fixed on any element → HTML_WIDGET. No exceptions.
delta.secondary_fix: Scroll listener JS → always move to Global Setup IIFE

fixed_nav_hard_rule.action: HTML_WIDGET — unconditional, no confidence threshold needed
fixed_nav_hard_rule.priority: HIGHEST — overrides all other classification attempts

issues: animation:marquee 22s linear infinite → HTML_WIDGET_ANIMATED should trigger
But naive engine only checks element's own computed animation, not keyframe names
@keyframes in separate <style> block → not associated with .marquee-track
Duplicate items not identified as intentional (naive engine may deduplicate content)
Classified as FLEX_ROW → loses animation entirely

reason: Has CSS @keyframes animation for horizontal movement
Duplicate content is intentional (seamless loop trick — remove at translateX(-50%))
overflow:hidden on wrapper is essential for the illusion
All must stay together as HTML_WIDGET
corrected_output.editability_note: Extract marquee items into a JavaScript array at top of widget for easy text editing

delta.root_cause: @keyframes in separate style block not linked to element with animation property
delta.fix: Animation resolver: cross-reference animation-name property values with @keyframes declarations in the same document

animation_cross_reference_rule.rule: When element has animation: [name] property, find @keyframes [name] in document
animation_cross_reference_rule.if_found_opacity_or_scale: May be OK as native widget with companion CSS animation

issues: Footer grid (2fr 1fr 1fr 1fr) detected as display:grid but collapsed to FLEX_COLUMN
fr unit values not parsed correctly — naive engine only handles repeat(N, 1fr)
footer-links <ul> → LIST_WIDGET (correct but loses hover styling)
footer-logo with <span> accent → TEXT_EDITOR (loses span colour)
status-dot with CSS animation → not detected as animated
footer-bottom flex row → FLEX_ROW container (correct)
footer-col-title h5 → HEADING_WIDGET h5 (correct)

corrected_output.brand_col: FLEX_COLUMN inner container — logo (HTML_WIDGET) + brand desc (TEXT_EDITOR)
corrected_output.nav_cols: FLEX_COLUMN inner containers — col-title (HEADING h5) + links (ICON_LIST widget)
corrected_output.footer_bottom: HTML_WIDGET — contains animated status dot (animation:pulse) + copyright
corrected_output.reason_for_footer_bottom_html: status-dot has CSS animation → footer-bottom as HTML_WIDGET
corrected_output.icon_list_for_nav: Use Icon List widget for footer nav links — editability 7/10, styleable via CSS class

delta.root_cause_1: Non-uniform fr grid (2fr 1fr 1fr 1fr) not parsed — only repeat(N,1fr) handled
delta.root_cause_2: Animated child in footer-bottom causes entire row to be classified incorrectly
delta.fix_1: CSS Grid fr parser: handle explicit fr values (2fr 1fr 1fr 1fr), not just uniform repeat()
delta.fix_2: Animated child rule: if one child of a FLEX_ROW has animation, that child → HTML_WIDGET, parent stays native

fr_unit_parser.description: Parse any CSS grid-template-columns value to extract fr proportions
patterns: 200px 1fr → mixed: note for companion CSS, use 1fr in Elementor with min-width on first col

animated_child_isolation_rule.rule: If a FLEX_ROW or FLEX_COLUMN child has animation/transition targeting its visual appearance, make ONLY THAT CHILD an HTML_WIDGET. Keep the parent container native.
animated_child_isolation_rule.exception: If the animated child is the layout-defining element (e.g., takes full width), make the entire parent HTML_WIDGET

corrected_output.container: FLEX_COLUMN with position:relative + overflow:hidden in companion CSS
corrected_output.watermark: ::before pseudo-element in companion CSS — MUST be explicitly generated
corrected_output.companion_css_entry: .nx-cta { position:relative; overflow:hidden; } .nx-cta > * { position:relative; z-index:1; } .nx-cta::before { content:'NEXUS'; position:absolute; right:-20px; top:50%; transform:translateY(-50%); ... }
corrected_output.content_value_extraction: The content:'NEXUS' string must be extracted from the CSS and placed in companion CSS — it is brand-specific, not a generic pattern

delta.root_cause: CSS pseudo-element declarations (::before, ::after) not included in companion CSS generation

pseudo_element_extractor_rules.trigger: CSS rule selector ending in ::before or ::after
pseudo_element_extractor_rules.action: Extract all properties, add to companion CSS under the element's nx- class

special_properties.content: May contain brand name string — preserve exactly
special_properties.position: absolute/fixed — check for stacking context conflicts
